3.why: It bounds both the outer and inner loops.

4.happening: Fix the first index i of the pair.
4.why: We'll pair it with every index that comes after it.
4.invariant: i is the first index of the pair under test.

5.happening: Pick a second index j after i.
5.why: Requiring j > i tries each unordered pair exactly once.
5.invariant: j is the second index of the pair.

6.happening: Does numbers[{i}] ({numbers[i]}) + numbers[{j}] ({numbers[j]}) equal the target ({target})?
6.why: We test every pair until one matches.
6.invariant: Exactly this pair is being checked.

7.why: This pair sums to the target.

loop.happening: Choosing the first index of the pair.
loop.why: Each element is tried as the first of a pair.
loop.invariant: i is the current first index.

move.why: Only indices after i form new pairs.
move.invariant: j runs from i+1 to the end.

check.happening: Comparing this pair's sum to the target.
check.why: We're scanning every pair for a match.

return.why: This pair sums to the target.
